title: Log Cabin Tavern, First and Second Floor Plans (HABS AR-32-10, sheet 1)
era_or_year: early-to-mid 19th century log construction; HABS drawings documented after 1933
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey; delineators P. V. Burton and H. M. Black
research_notes: Plans are sheet 1 (00001v.jpg, indexed 'Sheet 1 of 3') of HABS AR-32-10 'Drawings' (resource index 1; resource 0 is photographs). Note the drawing file sequence in this item starts at 00000v.
research_features: Simple two-story hewn-log dwelling with a central 'Open Hall' (dogtrot-style) passage, four rooms, a winding stair, and a porch. Construction notes call out hewn pine logs chinked with mud. Good small/vernacular + log-construction test case. Combined sheet: 1st and 2nd floor plans side by side.
feature_highlights: two-story hewn-log dwelling with first and second floor plans on one sheet
central OPEN HALL dogtrot passage on both floors
spiral/winding stair in the open hall with UP 16 R note
covered PORCH along one long side
four numbered rooms (101/102 below, 201/202 above) plus an old stair well
construction notes: hewn pine logs chinked with mud and bucks
removed-fireplace callouts and door swings at room openings
combined imperial and metric scale bar with a north arrow
supported_examples: straight log walls with thickness and junctions
single-swing door openings with swing arcs
a single active floor at a time
roadmap_examples: floor management (two stacked stories on one sheet)
wall construction profiles for hewn-log walls
covered-porch handling via the period vocabulary tracks

gap_features.why: the central OPEN HALL dogtrot passage and the side PORCH are roofed-but-open spaces, not fully enclosed rooms
the open-hall stair is a spiral/winding stair, not a straight-run stair
